figures.caption: Figure 1. Existing multimodal fusion strategies (left) often mix 2D and 3D features without explicit semantic disentanglement, leading to redundant and ambiguous representations. Our proposed shared-private fusion framework (right) separates modalityinvariant and modality-specific features, enabling efficient and interpretable 2D-3D interaction.
Figure 4. Visualization of object segmentation performance on the nuScenes validation set.
Figure 8. More visualization of object segmentation performance on the SemanticKitti Test set.
Figure 6. Representative failure cases on the nuScenes and Semantic KITTI validation datasets.
Figure 7. More visualization of object segmentation performance on the Nuscenes Test set.
Table 4. Comprehensive ablation study on backbone, fusion strategy, SAF query direction, and loss contributions.More detailed ablation studies are provided in Supplementary Section A (Table 7).

Figure 2. Overall architecture of the proposed UniD-Shift. The model takes synchronized 2D images and 3D point clouds as inputs. The 3D branch employs a SPTNet backbone to extract hierarchical geometric features, while the 2D branch utilizes a SAM-based encoder to obtain semantically enriched visual representations. Both modalities are decomposed into shared and private components, then feed into the Shared Attention Fusion (SAF) block into a unified latent representation.
Table 5. Cross-domain results on nuScenes (USA/Sing.). “Uni.” denotes uni-modal UDA, and “Cross.” denotes cross-modal UDA. Comparison highlights the effectiveness of multimodal adaptation.
Figure 3. Architecture of the proposed shared-private feature decomposition.
Figure 10. More visualizations comparing object segmentation performance with other methods on the SemanticKitti validation set.
Table 1. Quantitative results on the nuScenes validation set. Numbers for some baselines are taken from their original papers.
Figure 9. More visualizations comparing object segmentation performance with other methods on the nuScenes validation set.
